response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, testing-focused polyglot engineer with genuine low-latency systems experience, but he misses the role's hard minimums by a wide margin: roughly one year of professional C++ (National Instruments, 2014-2015) against a required eight, and about two years of large-scale distributed-systems work (Sociomantic) against a required five. His recent career trajectory is full-stack product engineering in TypeScript/Node and D, not cloud compute platforms, and he has no formal technical-leadership role mentoring cloud or DevOps engineers. Strong engineer, wrong profile for a C++ cloud compute technical lead.
strengths: Real high-throughput, low-latency systems experience (microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong engineering-quality culture: TDD, CI/CD, authored test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and ran testing/refactoring workshops
Demonstrated mentoring and knowledge-sharing through courses, conference talks, and founding/leading the open-source GISCollective platform
concerns: Far below the 8-year C++ minimum (~1 year professional C++; C++ absent from his skills list)
Large-scale infrastructure/distributed-systems experience totals roughly 2 years, short of the 5-year requirement, with most of it in D rather than C++
No formal technical-lead experience directing cloud or DevOps engineers; recent roles are individual-contributor full-stack product work
